Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jul-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: (Zoom)
Janet Brayer, 415.445.9555, Law Office of Janet Brayer for Plaintiff D 27 LLC.
Gary Sloboda, 415.461.1000, Bailey Law partners LLP for Defendant Marlene Casissa.

Argued. The defendant contests the tenative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MARLENE J. CASISSA, IN TRUST, MARLENE J. CASISSA, IN TRUST, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees is DENIED. The parties contracted to mediate the action in lieu of litigation to preserve their entitlement to recovery of fees. The parties further contracted that filing of a Court action in order to preserve the statute of limitations will not constitute a waiver or violation of mediation provisions. Despite multiple invitations to mediate the dispute starting April 2, 2020, defendant did not respond to any other them until January 5, 2021, 6 days before the action was dismissed and after significant amount of requested fees have been incurred. Based on the party's contract, Defendant should have proceeded to mediation (failure to respond to mediation request while proceeding with litigation is a tacit refusal) to preserve its entitlement to fees. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
